latest.title: Result of General Meeting and TVR
latest.content:
Today 14:26
RNS Number : 4558N
Huddled Group PLC
22 July 2026
Huddled Group plc
("Huddled", the "Company" or the "Group")
Result of General Meeting and TVR
Huddled Group plc (AIM:HUD), the circular economy e-commerce group, is pleased to confirm that at its General Meeting ("GM") held earlier today, all resolutions were duly passed.
The total number of proxy votes received on each resolution were as follows:
Resolution
For
%
Against
%
1
190,703,471
90.5%

90.2%
20,631,168
9.8%
4
190,035,246
90.2%
20,646,168
9.8%
At the GM, inter alia, a resolution was passed which granted the authorities to issue the remaining 290,971,715 Ordinary Shares
[1]
(the "New Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the subscription announced on 2 July 2026.
The funds raised will allow the Company to fulfil the next stage in its journey. As outlined in the RNS released on 30 June 2026, the Company spent the first six months of the year systematically improving the foundations of the business - improving unit economics, establishing next-day delivery as standard, and earning an 'Excellent' Trustpilot rating across a combined total of over 35,000 reviews across Peeko and Nutricircle.
Investment will be made into Nutricircle, improving its stock position as well as enhancing its website. The intention is to make Nutricircle the 'value' brand in the Health and Wellness sector, with a clear focus on three pillars: Nutrition, Nutraceuticals and Nootropics. This, alongside helping consumers shop by 'mission' such as sleep, focus and nutrition will, we believe, help us transition the brand to become the 'go-to' destination for health-conscious consumers looking for value.
On Peeko, we will focus on both improving our stock position as well as our channels to market, in terms of both customer acquisition and retention. Our ability to offer next-day delivery is a clear winner, we now need to use this, alongside our value offering, to attract and retain more customers.
Recent trials in 'live auction' shopping have proven to be very successful, with the brand now operating live auctions across both Whatnot and TikTok seven days a week. The intention is to broaden this out into additional categories over the coming weeks.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application has been made for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Admission is expected to take place on or around 23 July 2026. Upon Admission, the New Ordinary Shares will rank pari passu in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission and inclusive of indirect holdings, Martin Higginson, a director of the Company, will hold 13.23% of the Ordinary Shares in the Company.
Certain of the Company's shareholders are deemed to be acting in concert in accordance with The Takeover Code ("the Concert Party"). Following Admission, the Concert Party will hold 38.51% of the Ordinary Shares in the Company.
Following Admission, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company will be 811,305,717. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury.
The above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
Martin Higginson, Executive Chairman of Huddled Group plc, commented:
"We believe the additional capital raised will allow us not only to broaden our range of surplus products, it will also allow us to use this stock to entice more customers.
"Nutricircle will undertake a revamp with a clear focus on becoming a destination site for Health & Wellness customers looking for value.
"Peeko will use the improved stock position alongside its recent learnings in live shopping via Whatnot and TikTok to further enhance its shopping experience. It is clear live auction shopping excites customers and creates a sense of urgency. We believe this, combined with next-day delivery, gives us a real USP in the online shopping market."
Enquiries:

latest.title: Notice of GM
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0604L
Huddled Group PLC
06 July 2026
Huddled Group plc
("Huddled", the "Company" or the "Group")
Notice of General Meeting
Huddled Group plc (AIM:HUD), the circular economy e-commerce group announces that, further to the announcement on 2 July 2026, a notice of general meeting (the "GM") will be posted to shareholders today and will be available shortly on the Company's website: https://huddled.com/publications-and-reports/
The GM will be held at Token House, 11-12 Tokenhouse Yard, London, EC2R 7AS on 22 July 2026 at 2 p.m.
Background to and reason for the GM
On 30 June 2026, the Company announced (RNS No. 2597K) that it had raised gross funds of approximately £345,000 via the Initial Subscription and that it proposed to raise a further £899,000 by way of a conditional subscription, together with up to £100,000 by way of the Retail Offer, to be conducted through RetailBook (RNS No. 2617K). On 2 July 2026, the Company announced (RNS No. 6997K) that the Retail Offer had closed almost three times oversubscribed and had accordingly been increased to £200,000, and that the conditional subscription had been increased to approximately £964,000. In aggregate, therefore, the Fundraising, together with the Initial Subscription, will raise gross proceeds of approximately £1.51 million (before expenses).
The Issued Subscription Shares were issued and allotted on 30 June 2026 and were admitted to trading on AIM on 1 July 2026. Their issue and allotment exhausted all of the Company's existing share authorities and, as a consequence, the Company has insufficient authorities to issue the Subscription Shares and the Retail Offer Shares. The Company is therefore convening a General Meeting to request Shareholders to grant the authorities required to enable the New Ordinary Shares to be issued, and to refresh the authorities granted at the June 2026 AGM that were utilised to satisfy the issue and allotment of the Issued Subscription Shares.
This announcement should be read in conjunction with the full notice of GM sent to shareholders today. Capitalised terms used but not defined in this announcement have the meanings given to them in that document.
Enquiries:
For further information please visit www.huddled.com/investors, or contact:
Huddled Group plc

latest.title: Result of Oversubscribed Retail Offer & Fundraise
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6997K
Huddled Group PLC
02 July 2026
2 July 2026
Huddled Group plc
("
Huddled
", the "
Company
" or the "
Group
")
Result of Oversubscribed Retail Offer and completion of Subscription
Huddled (AIM: HUD), the circular economy e-commerce group, is pleased to announce that the Retail Offer, as announced on 30 June 2026 (the "
Retail Launch Announcement
"), has now closed. The Retail Offer, which was intended to raise £100,000, was almost three-times oversubscribed. The directors have therefore decided to upscale the Retail Offer by 100% to £200,000.
The Company is pleased to announce that following overwhelming support from both its Institutional Investors alongside its private shareholder base via the RetailBook Offer, the Company has successfully completed and closed the book at £1.51m gross of fees. This comprises £345,000 from the Firm Shares and AGM Conditional Shares already issued and admitted to trading, £964,000 via subscriptions and £200,000 via the RetailBook Offer at a price of 0.4 pence per share.
The directors would like to thank both their institutional and private client shareholder base for their continued support.
The Company will convene a General Meeting to seek shareholder approval for the issuance of the 240,971,715 Subscription Shares and 50,000,000 RetailBook Offer shares, totalling 290,971,715 shares (together, the "GM Conditional Shares"). Further details will be announced in due course.
The Company requires additional share authorities to allot the GM Conditional Shares. Accordingly, the Retail Offer is conditional, inter alia, upon the approval of the shareholders of the Company at a general meeting of the Company, and the Retail Offer Shares to be issued pursuant to the Retail Offer being admitted to trading on the AIM market operated by the London Stock Exchange ("Admission"). Completion of the Retail Offer is conditional, inter alia, upon the completion of the Subscription.
Capitalised terms used but not otherwise defined in this announcement shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Retail Launch Announcement unless the context requires otherwise.
Enquiries
Huddled Group plc
Martin Higginson
Dan Wortley
investors@huddled.com

A "Vote withheld" is not a vote in law and is not counted in the calculation of the percentage of shares voted "For" or "Against" any resolution.
Admission of Subscription Shares and Total Voting Rights
Following the successful passing of all resolutions at today's Annual General Meeting and further to the Company's announcement at 0700 on 30 June 2026 '
Share Subscription and Retail Offer
' (RNS No. 2597K), a total of 86,278,285 Ordinary Shares (being the Firm Shares and the AGM Conditional Shares) are to be admitted to trading on or around 1 July 2026.
Following Admission of the Firm Shares and AGM Conditional Shares, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company will be 520,334,002. The Company holds no shares in treasury. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for calculating whether they are required to notify their interest under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
The Firm Shares and AGM Conditional Shares, issued at 0.4 pence per share, represent approximately £345,000 of the £1,244,000 Subscription gross proceeds.
The Company will convene a General Meeting to seek shareholder approval for the issuance of the remaining 224,721,715 Subscription Shares, and the RetailBook Offer shares, up to 25,000,000, totalling up to 249,721,715 (together, the "GM Conditional Shares"). Further details will be announced in due course.
Definitions contained within his announcement have the same meaning as those contained within the Company's announcement at 0700 on 30 June 2026 '
Share Subscription and Retail Offer
' (RNS No. 2597K).

company_30s.what_they_do: HUD.L is a very small, publicly listed entity with no publicly disclosed core product or service line. The filings that are available are largely about corporate governance and capital structure rather than operations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Because it is a micro‑cap, any movement can be amplified by the limited liquidity. It sits in a sector that is often used by speculators to chase dramatic price swings, but there is no evidence that the company has a competitive moat or a sizable addressable market.
company_30s.current_state: The company appears to be in an early, cash‑burn phase with no revenue data or clear business plan in the public domain. Recent announcements focus on shareholder meetings and attempts to raise capital, suggesting it is still searching for a viable path forward.

rns_narrative: The first thread of activity is corporate governance: an AGM, a notice of a general meeting, and disclosure of shareholdings. This shows the board is actively managing ownership structure.

The second thread is capital raising: a retail book offer, an oversubscribed retail offer, and a share subscription. The company is trying to inject fresh cash, which could be a turning point if it succeeds.
The third thread is operational updates: full‑year results and a trading update. These give a snapshot of the company’s financial health, but the numbers are not yet disclosed in detail.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical silence with a few scattered bearish comments
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish chatter has surfaced; the few positive posts are generic and not tied to any concrete event.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Commentators worry about potential bankruptcy and a lack of clear business traction. One user noted the company is “priced for bankruptcy” and another highlighted a decline that needs evidence to reverse.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed largely unchanged; negative whispers persist while no new optimism has emerged.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity, signalling that the market is largely unaware of this stock.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the retail offer could bring a short‑term cash infusion that might stabilize the price.
If the company releases a clear earnings guidance that shows an improvement in cash flow or a realistic exit strategy, that could spark a bounce.
A significant increase in trading volume—especially if it coincides with a break above the current support level—would signal that smart money is entering.
expect_next.watch_for: Pay close attention to the next quarterly report for any sign of positive cash burn, and monitor the volume trend for a sustained uptick. A sudden spike in institutional buying would be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is likely still in the early stages of a potential crash. There is no evidence of a completed capitulation or a clear recovery path, so any mean‑reversion would have to be triggered by a substantive catalyst.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the lack of historical signal data and clear business traction makes a confident bullish stance premature.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the capital raise is successful and the company can demonstrate a realistic path to positive cash flow, the deep decline could set the stage for a dramatic rebound. A 100‑pence recovery to the mid‑level of the 52‑week range would represent a 200‑plus percent upside from the current floor.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s business model is unclear, and the repeated focus on fundraising suggests it is struggling to generate revenue. A failure to close the retail offer or an admission of insolvency would wipe out any upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside could exceed 200% if the company reverses, but the downside risk is essentially all the way to zero price if the business collapses. The asymmetry is high, but the probability of hitting the upside is very low without a clear catalyst.

playbook.if_bullish: None at present – there is no concrete signal to act on.
playbook.if_watching: Enter only if the retail offer closes successfully and volume begins to climb above the 20‑day average while the price touches the lower 52‑week support.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With no visible business traction, a failed capital raise and a dead market awareness, HUD.L remains a high‑risk, low‑reward proposition until a clear catalyst materialises.
